page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is a general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 CPU model operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. The instance is provisioned with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a balance between single-core efficiency and multi-core limitations. The instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance in compression tasks, achieving 30.29 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.33 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by a multi-core stress-ng score of 3401.18 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3232.0. Memory performance is mixed, with a strong read-only large block bandwidth of 56.38 GB/sec, but a weaker write bandwidth of 23.05 GB/sec. Complex workloads like LLM inference on a small 135M model show poor to weak throughput, with prompt processing at 148.1 tokens/sec and text generation at 68.36 tokens/sec.

The m8gb.large offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-performance multi-threaded scaling. Its dedicated ARMv9 cores provide predictable single-core execution, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic database operations, and text processing tasks. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, or memory-intensive database operations due to its lower multi-core benchmark ratings. Recommended workloads include lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale Redis caching.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is a general-purpose, arm64-based server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from an ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities, particularly in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, though multi-core scaling and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the m8gb.large is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight general-purpose workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, text processing, and modest database operations that do not require local NVMe storage or heavy multi-threaded compute resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is an arm64 general-purpose server with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 2.8 GHz ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.large is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz ARMv9 processor.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated ARMv9 cores and balanced memory for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic web serving.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor
8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression tasks
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic database operations
